title: Define the bounded Control Plane API vertical slice
goal: Produce a review-ready implementation contract for exposing the reviewed Control Plane v2 Registry through authorization-aware, project/task-scoped, bounded asynchronous API routes without changing frozen persistence semantics or starting UI work.

responsibilities: Map the frozen Registry operations to bounded API commands and projections
Define authorization, scope, validation, conflict, and event-loop boundaries
Specify acceptance and regression tests without modifying repository files
Review correctness, security, concurrency, rollback, and regression coverage
Reject any route that bypasses project/task scope or frozen Registry invariants
Review protocol, AI-DLC, reconciliation, evidence, approval, and Gate boundaries
Verify that the next action is safe, bounded, and does not invent the missing authoritative AI-DLC graph

workflow.title: Bounded API solution design
workflow.objective: Define the smallest implementation increment that exposes Registry create/read/evaluate/invalidate operations with explicit authorization and scope checks, request bounds, deterministic error mappings, async SQLite offload, and acceptance tests.
completion_conditions: Every proposed route names its authorization and project/task scope boundary
Mutation limits, optimistic conflicts, idempotency, and bounded error mappings are explicit
Synchronous SQLite operations are kept off the FastAPI event loop
Tests cover success, cross-scope refusal, invalid input, conflict, and rollback behavior
Frozen protocol and Registry persistence semantics remain unchanged
context_expectations: Use AGENTS.md and the checked-in architecture, schema, executable protocol, and PROGRESS sources as authority
Inspect current Registry, route, authorization, and test code rather than relying on prior transcripts
Treat the provisional methodology as a planning loop, not the authoritative AI-DLC graph
handoff_expectations: Return a concrete affected-file and route plan with risks and verification commands
Identify unknowns and blockers instead of inventing policy or evidence
Recommend exactly one bounded next safe implementation action

required_artifacts.description: A repository-grounded API design covering routes, command boundaries, data flow, limits, errors, and tests

required_evidence.description: Evidence that the design covers scope, bounds, asynchronous persistence, conflicts, rollback, and regression tests

gate_requirements.failure_action: Block and revise the solution design before independent review

workflow.title: Independent correctness and safety review
workflow.objective: Independently test the proposed design against repository behavior, security and scope isolation, SQLite transaction and event-loop safety, error truthfulness, and regression coverage.
completion_conditions: Authorization and project/task isolation cannot be bypassed
Mutation atomicity, idempotency, conflict behavior, and rollback are reviewable
API work cannot change frozen Registry semantics or treat exit/process success as semantic success
High and medium findings are absent or explicitly block the Task
context_expectations: Receive the Task contract and verified solution-design semantic result, not a full transcript
Inspect relevant executable code and tests independently
Treat missing implementation evidence as missing rather than passed
handoff_expectations: Return severity-ranked findings tied to concrete repository areas
State whether the design is implementable without weakening frozen invariants
Provide a bounded fix action for every blocker

required_artifacts.description: An independent correctness, security, concurrency, and regression review of the design

required_evidence.description: Evidence that no unresolved high or medium correctness and safety findings remain

gate_requirements.failure_action: Block and return the design for bounded correction

workflow.title: Protocol and methodology boundary review
workflow.objective: Verify that the design respects Agora-only state ownership, Context/Handoff boundaries, formal Artifact/Evidence/Approval/Gate separation, deterministic reconciliation, recovery safety, and the explicit deferral of UI and the missing authoritative AI-DLC graph.
completion_conditions: Agora remains the only writer of cross-runtime Task, Stage, and Gate state
Native runtime output remains an assertion until deterministic reconciliation
Artifacts, Evidence, Approvals, policy, state, and memory remain separate
No provisional workflow claim is presented as the authoritative AI-DLC graph
The implementation next action is bounded and recovery-safe
context_expectations: Receive the Task contract plus verified prior-stage semantic results
Use the protocol/domain freeze and executable models as normative authority
Do not infer methodology facts from screenshots, external notes, or chat history
handoff_expectations: Return explicit protocol or methodology blockers and their requirement identities
Separate missing authority from implementation defects
Recommend implementation only if the bounded design preserves all mandatory boundaries

required_artifacts.description: An independent protocol, methodology, reconciliation, and quality-Gate boundary review

required_evidence.description: Evidence that no unresolved high or medium protocol or methodology boundary findings remain

gate_requirements.failure_action: Block and revise the design without inventing missing methodology authority

acceptance_criteria: The Codex design produces a schema-valid semantic result grounded in current repository code
Claude independently reports no unresolved high or medium correctness or safety finding
Kiro independently reports no unresolved high or medium protocol, AI-DLC, reconciliation, or quality-Gate finding
The plan explicitly covers authorization, project/task scope, request bounds, conflict mapping, async SQLite offload, and acceptance tests
A human explicitly approves the reviewed plan before implementation begins
forbidden_constraints: Do not modify repository files during this planning and review loop
Do not start Task Workbench or other UI work
Do not change frozen protocol or Registry persistence semantics
Do not invent or rename the missing authoritative AI-DLC graph
Do not treat process exit code zero as semantic or Gate success
